title: How to make Lift ATX better — three product suggestions for the Austin gym
html:
How to make Lift ATX better — three product suggestions for the Austin gym

Source: 2025-12-20 Apple Note. Three bullets of unsolicited product feedback for Lift ATX — the $50/mo gym from the Aug 21 packlist that Yahir picked over Iron Forge. Modified through Feb 2027.

TL;DR

  • Three improvement bullets: updates and ads, TV for updates on gyms / events / promos that "peoples can sell," total revamp of website.
  • The note confirms Yahir picked Lift ATX (over Iron Forge Gym TX) — resolves the open question from the Aug packlist.
  • "TV for updates... that peoples can sell" is a whitelabel strategy-adjacent thought — Yahir is unprompted-pattern-matching to ad-as-sales-channel models, even at his neighborhood gym.
  • The 4-month modification window (Dec 2025 → Feb 2027) means Yahir kept opening the note over a year — this is active product thinking, not a one-shot dump.
  • Same impulse Yahir applies professionally to Rankett — see what's being underused in a system, propose the systemization. Operating-mode bleeding from work to gym.

Key quote

"TV for updates on gyms, events, promos that peoples can sell."

Pattern observed

Yahir is unable to stop operator-modeling even as a customer. The note is structurally the same as a Rankett strategy junk drawer entry — bullets of "what could this become if someone took it seriously." That he cares enough about Lift ATX to draft this suggests it's become a regular-presence node in the Austin life, not just a transactional gym membership.

The 4-month modification window also suggests this might be prep for a conversation — pitching the gym owner on changes, possibly trading work for membership. Same instinct as the Brotein / BroMenu gym-adjacent product line.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually pitch Lift ATX on these changes, or are they just internal annotations?
  • "Peoples can sell" — does this imply a referral/ad-revenue angle Yahir was thinking through?
  • Is the website-revamp suggestion the seed of an actual side-project (Rankett-style white-label for gyms)?
